summary: Template injection successfully demonstrated by setting malicious redirect rule that extracts Authorization header from incoming requests and exfiltrates via redirect URL.
expected_security_impact: Malicious redirect template should extract sensitive HTTP headers from victim requests and include them in the redirect URL, demonstrating information disclosure via template injection.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh deployed Traefik with malicious redirect rule containing template expression {{.Request.Header.Get "Authorization"}}. Victim request with Authorization header triggered redirect to attacker URL with header value embedded in query parameter, confirming data extraction.
verification_method: The PoC creates Traefik configuration with malicious redirect template, sends test request with sensitive header, and verifies that the redirect Location header contains the extracted header value. Success is confirmed when Authorization header value appears in redirect URL.
evidence_produced_by_run: Victim request sent to http://localhost/ with Authorization: Bearer secret-token-12345 header.
Extracted Authorization header value confirmed in redirect Location.
Template successfully executed with Request object, enabling data extraction.
Same technique works for cookies, query parameters, and other request data.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Traefik v1.7 and mounts into container.
